meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Israeli push for more force ‘not working out very well’
items.summary: “The Israeli public opinion is to use more force”

items.title: Two teenagers killed in Shiraz, two dead in Abu Dhabi as Iran war expands
items.summary: Deadly attacks rise as Israel announces strikes on Isfahan and as Trump and Tehran debate ceasefire terms.

items.title: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 27 of attacks?
items.summary: The US and Israel’s war on Iran is intensifying, as Trump again claims Iranian leaders want to 'make a deal'.

items.title: In U-turn, UK police say Palestine Action protesters will be arrested again
items.summary: London's Met Police had eased arrests following the High Court ruling that the ban on Palestine Action was unlawful.

items.title: Deaths and debts: Missiles in Gulf shake millions of South Asian families
items.summary: Most people killed in Iran's Gulf attacks have been from South Asia. And entire economies back home are on edge.

items.title: Senegal appeal to CAS against handing over of AFCON title to Morocco
items.summary: Senegal hope to overturn CAF's decision to crown Morocco champions after a walkout by Senegalese players in the final.

items.title: NBA owners vote to explore Seattle, Las Vegas expansion bids
items.summary: All 30 owners voted to explore the two city bids, according to ESPN, with the 2028-29 season the entry point.

items.title: Netanyahu boasts about ‘breaking boundaries in every sense of the word’
items.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says the country is working to expand its 'buffer zone' from Lebanon.

items.title: Oil prices rise higher as Iran denies US talks, dimming deescalation hopes
items.summary: Brent crude tops $104 a barrel as hopes fade for deescalation in US-Israel war on Iran.

items.title: Malaysia’s prime minister says Iran talks should end war
items.summary: Malaysia’s Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim said any talks with Iran must aim to end the war.

items.title: Videos show wave of missiles fired from Lebanon to Israel
items.summary: Videos show scenes of rocket interceptions in the sky over central Israel.

items.title: US lawmakers push for pause in data centres until AI safeguards in place
items.summary: Bernie Sanders and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez introduce bill to pause AI rollout amid growing backlash to technology.

items.title: Russia slams second US-Israeli strike at Iran’s Bushehr nuclear reactor
items.summary: Russia’s foreign ministry has accused the US and Israel of trying to spark a nuclear disaster after a strike at Bushehr.

items.title: Australia bans visitors from Iran amid war in the Middle East
items.summary: Australia's Home Affairs Department said decision to ban Iranian visitors amid the war was in the 'national interest'.

items.title: US jury finds Meta, Google, liable in social media addiction trial
items.summary: A Los Angeles jury has found Alphabet's Google and Meta liable for $6 million in a landmark social media addiction case.

items.title: Malaysia moves to tighten rules for expats, raising fears of talent flight
items.summary: Malaysia's government plans to slash the number of foreign workers to encourage local hiring and raise incomes.

items.title: Harrison: ‘There is going to be some kind of a military escalation.’
items.summary: Ross Harrison says public talk of diplomacy between the US and Iran is hiding hardline positions.

items.title: Tehran issues warning to regional neighbour if Iranian island occupied
items.summary: Iran's parliament speaker issues threat as US administration talks peace while committing more troops to conflict.

items.title: Trump says Iranian leaders want deal ‘so badly’ but fear retaliation
items.summary: Donald Trump said Iran is negotiating with the US but is “afraid to say it”.

items.title: Sarah Mullally enthroned as first female archbishop of Canterbury
items.summary: The first woman to lead the Church of England formally installed at ceremony before 2,000 guests.

items.title: Iran war live: US demands Tehran accept defeat; Israel pounds Lebanon
items.summary: Iranian official describes US's 15-point plan as 'maximalist' as state television outlines five conditions for peace.

items.title: Migrants march in southern Mexico to denounce immigration restrictions
items.summary: The march comes as a court in the US suggests there may be a secret deportation agreement between the US and Mexico.

items.title: Egypt says it is ready to host Iran de-escalation talks
items.summary: Egypt’s Foreign Minister Badr Abdelatty said Cairo is ready to host talks on Iran to support de-escalation.

items.title: Iranian foreign minister rejects talks with US
items.summary: “No negotiations have taken place.” Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi says Tehran is not in talks with the US.

items.title: Mexico will continue accepting Cuban medical workers despite US pressure
items.summary: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum praises services from Cuban doctors, who often work in underserved rural areas.
